question.text: RBI has mandated that for home loans taken from banks:

answer.text: There shall be no prepayment charges
Prepayment charges of 2% must be levied
Prepayment is not allowed in the first 3 years
Prepayment charges are decided by each bank independently

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RBI has mandated that there would be no prepayment charges for home loans taken from banks. This encourages borrowers to prepay when they have surplus funds, reducing their overall interest burden. **The Trap:** Some non-bank lenders (HFCs) may still charge prepayment fees. The RBI mandate specifically covers banks. **Cross-Exam Context:** RBI regulations on loan prepayment are tested in X-A (Ch 4). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Encourage clients with home loans to make annual prepayments — even one extra EMI per year can reduce tenure by several years.
